ab: makiteng ku diya kaadu mi i Valangaw, temakakesi ku lra mu, mukuwa mi kikaduwa i Tayhok, kadukadu mi lra i maci.
ch: 小時候我們家住在台東，上學以後，就搬到了台北，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Valangaw. After I started school, we moved to Taipei and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: temakesi ku lra i Daygak mu, temakesi ku kanantu cepa na Yuēncumin, madalram ku lra da ulra marekamelrimelri ta mauwan di ta kakuwayanan.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: semalra, kanini na kasede’an na derun mu, ingku di i valri na ma’inay muruma’ mi i dekalr kiumaumalr kana dawdawan, maranger ku madalram data kakuwayanan inta.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: mulavat lra da makawalun tu ’ami ini na dawdawan, aymu, mena’u ta lralrak diya tu pinidadekan.
ch: 這老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: kiumalr ku kantaw da sayhu marengay da eman ngaiyan? nu temarakasavakan salaw ’ilrepat, di sayhu diya tamarapayrang, tamaramingkok di temaradipung.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的建和語說得很流利，也會說閩南語、中國語和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Kasavakan, as well as Hokkien, Chinese and Japanese.

ab: kiumalr ku lra madalram ku lra, mau na makiteng diya mu, temakesi kana Dipung, di mituruma’an da payrang, di kanantu kiyakarunan marekadalram kana Rutiya, semalra sadu lra tu kinasayhuwan da piningaiyan.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是閩南人，而他工作上認識了外省人，所以他所懂得語言好多。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Hokkien, and he knows many Chinese at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: madayadayar mi mu, sadu tu rinarengay na dawdawan kanantu ikadalraman da kakuwayanan a pa’a’alrupan.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: ara adiya mi karuwa madalram penauwa datu rinarengay, aymu, niyam pasirepay kana Soci, nu muruma’ mi lra maruwa mi diya temarakinger.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: makiteng ku diya adi ku kamadalraman data kakuwayanan. puremasamad ku kana mau ku na Yuēncumin.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，而對於自己是原住民感到有些不好意思自卑。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: marekasadu lra nangku kinadalraman mu, marekasadu lra ku kinapakumau data kirekamelriyan a pinuyumayan, di mau na pakamacas data anger awlra!
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為卑南族人，是很獨特的， 也是讓我們值得驕傲的吧！
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a pinuyumayan is unique and something to be proud of.
